source_citation: Baerson et al. 2002 Plant Physiology 129:1265-1275; PMID 12114580
notes: Peer-reviewed GenBank-linked accession pair: PubMed metadata lists AJ417033/AJ417034; GenBank records label AJ417033 as epsps-R and AJ417034 as epsps-S. Baerson reports mature-protein Pro106Ser; GenBank translated partial-protein difference is position 107 (resistant=Ser, susceptible=Pro), mapped by local alignment to 8UMJ maize sequence position 107 / PDB residue 106. Correction note: 8UMJ includes an initiating MET numbered 0, so FASTA sequence position 107 corresponds to PDB residue 106, not 108. AJ417033 also carries a second translated difference at position 382 (resistant=Leu, susceptible=Pro), but Baerson reports that the second substitution does not contribute significantly to reduced glyphosate sensitivity.

column16: Real weed-evolved allele (Malaysia field population) confirmed via GenBank KM078728 (full-length resistant EPSPS cDNA). Paper's own 'plant EPSPS numbering' conserved motif (95-LFLGNAGTAMRPL-107) places Thr at position 102 and Pro at 106, the same convention already used for Pro106Ser in this table - independently confirmed against this project's own epsps_8umj_distance_sasa.csv, which already lists PDB residue 102 as THR with no extra offset needed. CAVEAT (confidence=medium): T102I has never been observed or shown viable as a standalone mutation in this or any other reported population - it exists only as part of the TIPS double mutation with Pro106Ser in the same allele. The paper's own recombinant-enzyme kinetics were only measured for WT, P106S-alone, and the TIPS double; the authors state T102I alone would likely be unfit/non-viable, explaining why it has never been observed in isolation in nature. Included here as an accepted, real, weed-evolved TSR-associated structural position (not an engineered/lab construct), but its independent causal contribution is not isolated, analogous to the ALS Ala122Ser/A282D confound already documented in als_mutations.csv. No wt_accession is given because the paper does not deposit a separate paired susceptible sequence beyond the reference WT EPSPS coding sequence used for recombinant expression. Gly101Ala was considered and explicitly excluded - see docs/DECISION_LOG.md - it is documented in the literature almost exclusively as an engineered/transgenic substitution (e.g. petunia/canola/maize biotech patents), not as a weed-evolved TSR mutation; the Eleusine indica primary-source papers checked for this project explicitly report Gly101 as unmutated in their resistant field populations.
